name: Specs Powell
abstract: Gordon "Specs" Powell (June 5, 1922 – September 15, 2007) was an American jazz drummer who began performing in the swing era.

concerts.label: Bel Canto Foundation Jazz Concert to Benefit Musicians

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: We had all different rhythms on this other one that I did. Well one of them was that "Big Noise from Winnetka." See, and all I wanted Specs Powell to just play the tympany...

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Bert
about_quotes.evidence: We were two days in George Simon’s house recording... Specs Powell and Hank Cohoot on piano.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Bert interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Red Norvo
about_quotes.evidence: I had Shorty Rogers, Eddie Bert, Aaron Sax, Hank Kahout, Specs Powell, Clyde Lombardi.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Maxwell
about_quotes.evidence: I think the only staff musicians on that show was Specs Powell and myself.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: The text mentions Gordon 'Specs' Powell as part of Eddie South's orchestra in a descriptive passage about the club's lineup, but the interviewee does not explicitly state a personal interaction or relationship with Powell.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmie Crawford
about_quotes.evidence: There were two, Specs Powell and Jimmy Crawford, two drummers... Yeah, Specs was a very good professional drummer... me, I wasn't and that was one of the reasons I started studying, to try to get like him.
